reasoning: The Lean statement assumes G is a finite group with `card G = 312` and claims there exists a prime p, with p dividing card G, and a Sylow p-subgroup P of G that is normal. This directly matches the informal statement "a group of order 312 has a normal Sylow p-subgroup for some prime p dividing its order." The Mathlib `Sylow p G` structure represents maximal p-subgroups, and combined with the `p.Prime` hypothesis captures the standard notion of a Sylow p-subgroup. The `P.Normal` predicate corresponds to normality of the underlying subgroup, and the quantifier structure (∃ p, ∃ P, ...) faithfully matches "for some prime p ... normal Sylow p-subgroup".
